text.title: Multi Project Allocation Tracker Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Multi Project Allocation Tracker methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every active person produces exactly one allocation row per cycle; rows MUST sum project allocations + non-project time (PTO, ops, internal) to the person's capacity.
rule.rationale: missing rows or unbalanced sums hide real conflicts and burnout risk.

rule.statement: Any row where total_allocated > 1.0 of capacity MUST emit an explicit over_allocation_flag with delta and proposed mitigation; silent over-allocation is rejected.
rule.rationale: silent over-allocation is the #1 leading indicator of slipped milestones.

rule.statement: Any row where total_allocated < 0.6 of capacity MUST emit an under_allocation_flag with proposed addition or a benched-with-reason note; un-flagged under-allocation is rejected.
rule.rationale: under-allocation hidden in 'misc' burns margin invisibly.

rule.statement: Every per-project allocation MUST cite a source (project plan, ticket assignment, person's confirmed agreement); un-sourced allocations are flagged 'verify-before-commit'.
rule.rationale: agent-driven allocations invent plausible splits; anchoring keeps them auditable.

rule.statement: Every over/under flag MUST carry at least one concrete rebalance proposal with named owner of the decision.
rule.rationale: flags without proposals decay into wallpaper.
